channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: [7] Controllable hydro-thermoelastic heat transport in ultrathin semiconductors at room temperature
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij

Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Heat transport in relevant 2D semiconductor materials (MoSe2/MoS2).

Tags: #2Dmaterials #transport #semiconductors

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03297-1 The combination of viscous heat flow and thermoelastic effects leads to a non-diffusive heat transport regime in MoSe2 and MoS2. Moreover, it can be controlled through the variation in sample thickness and by choosing between continuous and pulsed heating.
item.dc:title: [7] Controllable hydro-thermoelastic heat transport in ultrathin semiconductors at room temperature
item.dc:creator: Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij; Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.
J. Tur-Prats
J. D. Mehew
D. Saleta Reig
R. Farris
J. Camacho
J. A. Haibeh
A. Sokolov
P. Ordejón
S. Huberman
A. Beardo
F. X. Alvarez
K. J. Tielrooij
item.description:
Last authors: F. X. Alvarez, K. J. Tielrooij

Authors: S. Varghese, J. Tur-Prats, J. D. Mehew, et al.

Score 7/10 B_IMPORTANT_CONDMAT

Why: Heat transport in relevant 2D semiconductor materials (MoSe2/MoS2).

Tags: #2Dmaterials #transport #semiconductors

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03297-1 The combination of viscous heat flow and thermoelastic effects leads to a non-diffusive heat transport regime in MoSe2 and MoS2. Moreover, it can be controlled through the variation in sample thickness and by choosing between continuous and pulsed heating.

item.title: [9] Spatially anisotropic Kondo resonance coupled with the superconducting gap in a kagome metal
item.content:encoded:
Last authors: Ziqiang Wang, Hong-Jun Gao

Authors: Zichen Huang, Hui Chen, Zhongqin Zhang, et al.

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: kagome metal

Tags: #kagome #Kondo #superconduct

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03292-6 How magnetic impurities influence superconductivity and electronic order in kagome metals remains unclear. Now anisotropic Kondo resonances intertwined with the superconducting gap are observed in a magnetically doped kagome superconductor.
item.dc:title: [9] Spatially anisotropic Kondo resonance coupled with the superconducting gap in a kagome metal
item.dc:creator: Last authors: Ziqiang Wang, Hong-Jun Gao; Authors: Zichen Huang, Hui Chen, Zhongqin Zhang, et al.
Hui Chen
Zhongqin Zhang
Hao Zhang
Zhen Zhao
Ruwen Wang
Haitao Yang
Wei Ji
Ziqiang Wang
Hong-Jun Gao
item.description:
Last authors: Ziqiang Wang, Hong-Jun Gao

Authors: Zichen Huang, Hui Chen, Zhongqin Zhang, et al.

Score 9/10 A_MUST_READ

Why: title strong match: kagome metal

Tags: #kagome #Kondo #superconduct

Abstract: Nature Physics, Published online: 15 May 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-026-03292-6 How magnetic impurities influence superconductivity and electronic order in kagome metals remains unclear. Now anisotropic Kondo resonances intertwined with the superconducting gap are observed in a magnetically doped kagome superconductor.
